parameters.formDescription: Research books by natural language topic or by specific ASINs. Use 'topic' mode to describe a subject; use 'asins' mode to provide specific Amazon product IDs.

values.placeholder: e.g. documentary filmmaking, British trivia quiz books, sourdough bread recipes
ASINs (comma-separated, e.g. B0GTQQNVVZ,B09XYZ) — only used in asins mode

parameters.jsCode:
const j = $input.first().json;
return [{ json: { status:'complete', report_path: j.filepath, report_size_bytes: j.reportLength, message:`Report saved to ${j.filepath}` } }];

_meta.notes: Upgraded from BRI-885: swapped Firecrawl from self-hosted (http://host.docker.internal:3002) to Firecrawl Cloud API (https://api.firecrawl.dev). API key now uses real cloud key (fc-ef...) instead of fc-brilliantio-local. Both scrape nodes updated to use HTTPS and cloud endpoint. Self-hosted Firecrawl at localhost:3002 is preserved (BRI-897 will handle removal).

proxy_setup_instructions.status: MANUAL STEPS REQUIRED — proxy account cannot be created programmatically
proxy_setup_instructions.summary: Firecrawl currently has no residential proxy configured. Amazon product page scraping may be blocked by bot protection. To fix this, complete the DataImpulse proxy setup below.
steps: 1. Sign up at https://dataimpulse.com (pay-as-you-go, ~$1/GB, credits never expire). Purchase minimum $5-10 credit.
2. Obtain proxy credentials from your DataImpulse dashboard: host, port, username, password.
3. SSH to the VPS, then edit /opt/firecrawl/.env and add the following three lines (replace placeholders with your actual credentials):
4. Edit /opt/firecrawl/docker-compose.yml — add proxy env vars to the puppeteer-service container's environment block:
5. Restart Firecrawl: cd /opt/firecrawl && docker compose down && docker compose up -d

6. Test by running this workflow in topic mode with 'documentary filmmaking', market=uk, max_results=10. Without a proxy, the Scrape Category & Parse ASINs node will throw an error with a reminder to complete these steps.
